summary: Matrix recording of the emotional 9/11/90 Spectrum show captures a soaring He's Gone → Spoonful sequence and solid overall sound despite some audience‑sourced sections.
review: The performance is remembered for its heartfelt energy after Brent Mydland’s death, highlighted by an inspired He’s Gone › Spoonful jam that many reviewers call "perfect" or "spooky."  The sbd‑matrix source provides a generally good mix—crisp soundboard on most of the set with audience bleed on China Cat › Rider—but vocal clarity is occasionally weak.  Overall the show is praised as one of the best of the early Vince era, though a few listeners note an uneven first set and mixed feelings about the recording quality.

ai_rating.rationale: Average user rating is ~3.6/5, but most comments praise the performance (especially He’s Gone → Spoonful) and describe the recording as decent to good; weighting favors show quality, yielding a solid 4‑star overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Mostly soundboard clarity with occasional audience bleed (notably China Cat › Rider). Vocals are described as weak in places, but the mix is generally enjoyable and captures the venue ambience.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is viewed as solid but not spectacular, while the second set delivers an emotional arc—starting with a powerful He’s Gone › Spoonful jam and flowing through evocative numbers that reflect the band’s grieving mood.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for being "on fire," delivering emotive vocals and searing guitar work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for jumping in with bass during the He’s Gone/Spoonful jam, supporting Jerry's improvisations.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince Welnick is highlighted as fitting in well, providing solid keyboard textures and a solo that many felt honored Brent’s legacy.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart’s birthday is mentioned; drums are described as strong and “evil best” during the jam.
